attacking_style: Pure sprinter who launches from screened or sub-optimal positions in final 200m. Thrives in technical, compressed finales where positioning chaos creates opportunities rather than textbook leadout trains.
team_tactics: Team provides bridging support on climbs/rolling terrain rather than traditional sprint trains. Multiple observations show domestiques expending resources to bring him back after pace attacks, then improvising leadout after crashes/positioning failures.
terrain_preference: Rolling terrain with selective climbs where UAE-style pace attacks force selection but allow regrouping. Technical finales with compression suit his ability to find gaps. Headwind sprints showcase raw power beyond pure speed.
vulnerability: Dependent on teammates for bridging on climbs when pace attacks occur—without De Koenig-type domestiques to chase back, could be dropped. Leadout crashes force solo navigation, though he's proven adaptable.
key_patterns: Won sprints after losing full/partial leadout to crashes (3 observations), showing elite self-navigation ability
Survived climbing pace attacks then regrouped for sprint finish (2 observations on rolling Vuelta stages)
Wins from screened/boxed positions rather than open leadout launches (2+ technical finale victories)
Team prioritizes bridging/survival over traditional leadout infrastructure in selective stages
Headwind sprint power exceeds reputation as pure speed sprinter (Tour 2022 demonstration)
form_trajectory: positive: 2022 Tour performance showed evolution beyond pure leadout dependency, winning headwind sprint solo. Confidence fully restored post-injury by 2021 Vuelta, beating top-tier field repeatedly.
